specific_location: Along an irrigation supply ditch about half way between LCR 52 and 56 and due west of Cobb Lake.
description: A little smaller and much more slender than a robin. A bright yellow belly and a solid gray throat and chest. Large, black thick bill. Grayish-green back. Two pale white wingbars, and a tail that showed considerable red when it flew.Behaviors: We observed it perched with an insect in its mouth, and we watched it fly three different times when it was disturbed by our presence.Call: Call heard by Nancy Bell: a clear, single rising syllable. Same as Great Crested Flycatcher calls heard many times a month ago while birding in South Carolina.Plumage: adult
